rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports, using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contracts, with component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: In component type contracts, assume input envelopes and guarantee output ranges only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Ensure AGREE expression types match AADL port types; avoid mismatched type assumptions.
Do not encode AADL properties like timing or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq declarations for boolean range checks: eq var : bool = x >= min and x <= max;
AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
Do not generate range assumptions or guarantees without visible feature names or concrete bounds.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens
Implementation-level subcomponent paths in type contracts
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
